items.title: India-UK FTA: Kolkata flags off first jewellery export consignment to UK under CETA
items.summary: Kolkata dispatched its first jewellery export to the United Kingdom under the India-UK CETA. This marks the city's entry into the first nationwide shipment following the pact's implementation. Indian gem and jewellery exporters now have zero-duty access to the UK market. This agreement is expected to significantly boost India's jewellery exports to the UK. Kolkata is poised to benefit from this transformative trade opportunity.

items.summary: Market strategist Rohit Srivastava predicts the Nifty 50 index could reach 27,000 by the end of this year or early next year. He advises investors to buy dips and focus on growth stocks despite geopolitical uncertainties that threaten short-term stability.

items.title: 99% of exports to become duty-free with India-UK trade treaty: Amit Shah
items.summary: The India-UK free trade agreement begins today, offering zero-duty access for ninety-nine percent of Indian exports. This pact will unlock untapped potential across many labour-intensive industries and sectors. Indian professionals will also receive significant exemptions while working in the United Kingdom. Trade between the two nations is expected to double by the year 2030. This agreement is seen as a key step towards a developed India.

items.title: Tata Motors PV flags concern over proposed direct credit buys from BEE in CAFE II shifts
items.summary: Tata Motors has voiced concerns regarding a government proposal on CAFE II compliance credits. The company believes allowing direct credit purchases from the Bureau of Energy Efficiency could weaken the framework. Tata Motors insists the BEE should not originate or sell credits in its own capacity. This provision could offer a cheaper compliance option than statutory penalties. The manufacturer argues this undermines the technology-driving purpose of the CAFE regime.

items.title: India approves Rs 62,500 crore Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme (MPMS) to boost exports and jobs
items.summary: The union cabinet approved a new mobile phone manufacturing scheme. This initiative has an outlay of sixty-two thousand five hundred crore rupees. The scheme aims to boost domestic production and exports significantly. It also targets generating substantial direct employment opportunities. This plan will promote technological sovereignty and Indian brand development.

items.title: HDB Financial Q1 Results: Profit jumps 38% YoY to Rs 785 crore; NII up 20%
items.summary: HDB Financial Q1 Results: Net interest income rose 20% to Rs 2,509 crore from Rs 2,092 crore a year earlier. Net total income increased 17% to Rs 3,185 crore from Rs 2,726 crore in the year-ago quarter.

items.summary: Managed workspace provider IndiQube has leased 390,000 sq. ft. in Sector 142, Noida, marking its largest campus in the NCR. The expansion comes as the Noida Expressway emerges as a key office hub, with rising demand from GCCs and technology firms amid record growth in India's flexible office leasing market.

items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Share Prices Updates: BSE Sensex closed 130.49 pts or 0.17% positive at 77,185.43, and Nifty 50 was up 26.45 pts or 0.11% to 24,078.50.

items.title: Cabinet clears Rs 25,500 crore highway push to decongest Varanasi
items.summary: The Union Cabinet approved two highway projects for Varanasi. These projects will cost nearly Rs 25,500 crore in total. One project connects NH-31 and Varanasi Ring Road along River Varuna. The second project links NH-19 and Varanasi Ring Road along River Ganga. Both initiatives aim to significantly decongest the city and improve connectivity.

items.title: Apple Intelligence cleared for use on iPhones in China after regulatory approval
items.summary: China's cyberspace regulator announced that Apple Intelligence, a generative AI service, has been registered for iPhones. It will integrate Baidu and Alibaba's AI capabilities, though no rollout date was provided. Details here.

items.title: India's June unemployment rate at 5.5% y/y
items.summary: A Reuters poll had projected the June unemployment rate at 5.4%.

items.title: India-UK FTA to create new opportunities for Indian businesses, labour-intensive sectors: Industry
items.summary: India and the United Kingdom operationalized a free trade agreement on Wednesday. This pact grants zero-duty access for nearly ninety-nine percent of India's exports. Labour-intensive sectors like textiles and leather will see enhanced competitiveness. The agreement also provides greater certainty and transparent rules for businesses. This landmark pact strengthens bilateral economic relations and creates new opportunities.

items.title: Digit Life Insurance enters India’s retirement solutions market
items.summary: Go Digit Life Insurance has entered India's retirement solutions segment. The insurer is betting on growing demand for pension and annuity products. This move expands their offerings beyond protection and savings products. The company launched the Digit Life Pension Plan for customers. This plan offers guaranteed and market-linked income options.

items.summary: Brokerages highlighted a sharp margin contraction and uncertainty in key business segments.

items.title: First India-UK CETA export consignments flagged off from Hyderabad
items.summary: India and the United Kingdom launched their comprehensive economic and trade agreement. First export consignments from Hyderabad to the UK were flagged off today. This agreement allows many domestic goods to enter the British market duty-free. Telangana is expected to benefit significantly from expanded trade opportunities. Exporters can now access new markets and strengthen their global presence.

items.summary: Small-cap FMCG stock Spice Lounge Food Works hit its upper circuit on the BSE on Wednesday, 15 July, a day after the company announced it had opened its first Wing Zone Outlet in Bengaluru.

items.title: HDFC AMC Q1 Results: Net profit rises 12% to Rs 837 crore, revenue up 14%
items.summary: HDFC Asset Management Company reported a 12% rise in Q1 FY27 net profit to ₹837 crore, supported by higher revenue and asset growth. Quarterly average AUM climbed 13% year-on-year, while shares gained over 2% after the earnings announcement.

items.title: How to crack a 'great deal' with India? UK reveals details of dealing with 'phenomenal negotiators'
items.summary: UK officials described securing the India-UK Free Trade Agreement as a tough negotiation. They praised India's phenomenal negotiating team and Minister Piyush Goyal's skills. Bilateral trade is expected to increase by over 25 billion pounds annually. This pact aims to reduce tariffs and improve market access for businesses. Both nations remain committed to addressing concerns through established legal processes.

items.title: Nifty 50 above 27,000? PL Capital raises target, sees more upside in Indian stock markets but flags key risks
items.summary: PL Capital has raised its one-year Nifty 50 target to 27,019, citing improved macroeconomic conditions, resilient demand, and easing oil prices, while cautioning about risks from geopolitical tensions and potential El Niño effects on markets and earnings.

items.title: Rupee ends flat as merchant bids, oil blunt comfort from modest inflows
items.summary: The Indian rupee hovered close to a one-month low on Wednesday, driven by modest portfolio inflows and increased corporate demand for dollars. Asian currencies remained mostly stable, with the dollar index resting near 101. Meanwhile, oil prices rose due to rising geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, prompting market participants to keep a close watch on inflation data and potential changes in central bank rates.

items.title: India, UAE central banks said to hold talks on FX deposit issues
items.summary: India and the UAE central banks have discussed regulatory hurdles affecting India's drive to attract foreign-currency deposits from NRIs in the Gulf. The talks come as Indian banks seek up to $50 billion under the RBI's subsidised deposit scheme, while concerns over capital outflows, due diligence and funding costs continue to pose challenges.

items.title: Treasury yields rise as investors await latest producer price inflation data
items.summary: Treasury yields were edged higher as investors awaited the latest producer price inflation print for June.

items.title: Several flights diverted after plane blocks Gatwick runway
items.summary: An Easyjet flight had to land at Stanstead Airport instead, but then became stuck there when it couldn't find fuel.

items.title: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
items.summary: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

items.title: Russian oil still India's key energy bet despite looming US tariff risks
items.summary: Proposed US tariffs on Russian crude imports could inject fresh market volatility. Replacing Russian supply would be difficult due to limited spare production capacity. US senators unveiled a revised sanctions bill with softened tariffs on energy buyers. India's Russian crude imports hit record levels, acting as a stabilizing force. Disrupting Russian exports could tighten the already constrained global oil market.

items.title: India-UK FTA takes off with tariff cuts, London targets $100 bn trade from 'gold standard' deal
items.summary: The India-UK Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement has officially come into force. This pact aims to significantly increase bilateral trade between the two nations. Nearly ninety-nine percent of India's exports will now receive zero-duty market access. British exports to India will also benefit from lower or zero tariffs. This agreement is expected to foster greater investment and economic cooperation.

items.title: 'We faltered, did not move quickly:' How IBM CEO Arvind Krishna's statement led to $70 billion wipeout
items.summary: IBM shares rebounded nearly 2% in premarket trade after plunging 25% in their worst single-day fall in 58 years, wiping out nearly $70 billion in market value. The selloff was triggered by weaker-than-expected preliminary Q2 results and concerns that customers are shifting spending from IBM's traditional businesses to AI infrastructure.

items.title: Deepinder Goyal's next big bets: $1,000 forehead wearable, $20 mn aviation investment after Zomato
items.summary: Deepinder Goyal, co-founder of Zomato, is launching a wearable device called Temple to track metabolic rates in real time. Goyal also invests in LAT Aerospace, aiming to connect smaller airstrips with low-cost aircraft.

items.title: Midnight social media curfew and limits to infinite scrolling proposed for older UK teens
items.summary: The U.K. government has proposed new measures to protect older teens on social media, including a midnight curfew and a limit to infinite scrolling.

items.title: Andhra Pradesh Minister Nara Lokesh lays foundation for Rs 3,100-crore cement unit in Kadapa
items.summary: Andhra Pradesh IT Minister Nara Lokesh laid the foundation for Dalmia Bharat's new cement unit. This Rs 3,100-crore facility will be the company's largest in South India. The green cement unit is expected to be commissioned by the third quarter of 2027-28. It will operate on over eighty percent renewable power and recycle all its water. This significant project will also create one thousand new employment opportunities.

items.title: NSE, Augmont partner to expand Electronic Gold Receipts ecosystem
items.summary: NSE has partnered with Augmont Enterprises to expand the Electronic Gold Receipts (EGR) ecosystem, aiming to unlock India's vast household gold holdings. The initiative seeks to improve liquidity, enable exchange-traded ownership of physical gold, reduce import dependence and create a transparent, SEBI-regulated marketplace for the precious metal.

items.title: HDFC AMC Q1 Results: Net profit jumps 12% YoY to ₹837 crore; revenue surges 14%
items.summary: HDFC AMC Q1 Results: HDFC AMC reported a 12% YoY increase in Q1 net profit to ₹837 crore and a 13.6% rise in revenue to ₹1,100 crore. Total expenses rose 27% to ₹271 crore, while average AUM grew 11.2% YoY, indicating strong performance in the mutual fund sector.

items.title: UK-India FTA to boost economic ties; British Airways to increase weekly flights to India by winter
items.summary: The UK-India Free Trade Agreement (FTA) is expected to unlock new business opportunities and deepen bilateral economic engagement, prompting British Airways to expand its operations in the Indian market. The airline will increase its weekly flights to India from 63 to 70 during the winter schedule, reflecting growing confidence in the strengthening economic ties between the two countries, British Airways General Manager David Wright said.

items.title: ICICI Pru Life Q1 Result: Net profit jumps 28% YoY to Rs 386 crore
items.summary: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance reported a twenty-eight percent net profit increase. Net premium income rose to nine thousand seven hundred forty-nine crore rupees. New business premium grew by twenty-one percent, and policy numbers also increased. Total revenue reached twenty-eight thousand three hundred ninety-five crore rupees. The company proposed changing its name to ICICI Life Insurance Ltd.

items.title: ASHVINI set to invite bids for construction of Rs 28,000-cr Rajasthan nuclear power project
items.summary: ASHVINI will soon invite bids for a significant nuclear island EPC package. This package is for four 700 MW reactors at the Mahi Banswara Rajasthan atomic power project. The tender covers engineering, manufacturing, supply, and construction for the nuclear island systems. This initiative supports India's accelerated nuclear expansion program and its energy mission. The project will add substantial nuclear generation capacity and strengthen domestic manufacturing capabilities.

items.title: NSE partners with Augmont to boost Electronic Gold Receipts ecosystem
items.summary: Electronic Gold Receipts, launched by NSE in May, enable physical gold to be converted into exchange-traded, dematerialised securities held in investors’ demat accounts

items.title: Cabinet approves India Semiconductor Mission 2.0; earmarks Rs 1.27 lakh crore for the project
items.summary: The Union Cabinet approved India Semiconductor Mission 2.0 with significant funding. A Rs 62,500 crore Mobile Phone Manufacturing Scheme was also cleared. These initiatives aim to boost domestic electronics manufacturing capabilities. The approvals are part of seven key decisions worth over ₹2.19 lakh crore. These measures will strengthen India's global supply chain position.

items.title: Thames Water returns to profit after raising bills
items.summary: But the firm says it only has enough debt funding to keep the business going until the end of 2026.

items.title: Nifty Pharma jumps 14% YTD, outshines Nifty 50; is there more upside ahead?
items.summary: The Nifty Pharma index has surged 14.53% YTD, outperforming the Nifty 50's 7.87% decline. Key drivers include improving earnings visibility and stable demand in domestic formulations, while geopolitical tensions and inflation concerns weigh on broader market sentiment.

items.title: Global Market: ASML raises 2026 outlook as AI boom drives strong earnings, plans capacity expansion
items.summary: ASML raised its 2026 revenue guidance to €43-45 billion, up from €36-40 billion, after beating Q2 estimates on surging AI chipmaking demand, sending shares up 5.7% as the company plans a 30% capacity boost for its EUV systems over the next two years.

items.title: Karnataka comes up with draft apartment law to mandate safety audits, streamline ownership
items.summary: Karnataka has unveiled a draft bill to modernize apartment ownership and management laws. This legislation aims to replace outdated acts and improve urban housing governance. It mandates structural stability checks for older apartment buildings every five years. The bill also introduces a dispute resolution mechanism and clarifies undivided share calculations. Public suggestions are invited before the finalization of this important new law.

items.title: Karnataka proposes structural audits for ageing apartments under new ownership law
items.summary: Karnataka unveiled a draft law for mandatory structural stability certification for apartment buildings. This certification will be required after thirty years and then every five years. The proposed bill also addresses undivided share calculations and resident rights. It mandates forming an association within three months of booking half the apartments. The government seeks suggestions on this draft legislation by August sixth.

items.title: Market strategy: Kotak Equities adds TCS, SBI Life Insurance in model portfolio, raises weight on L&T
items.summary: Market strategy: Both TCS and SBI Life have been assigned a weight of 150 basis points (bps) in the brokerage’s model portfolio. The brokerage, however, clarified that TCS's inclusion in the model portfolio should not be interpreted as a broader endorsement of the IT services sector.

items.title: Mukul Agrawal trims stake in this smallcap infra firm, likely exits Surya Roshni in Q1. Do you own?
items.summary: Ace investor Mukul Agrawal trimmed his stake in J Kumar Infraprojects and is likely to have exited Surya Roshni during the June quarter, according to the latest shareholding disclosures. While he reduced his holding in the construction company, his name no longer appears among Surya Roshni's key shareholders, indicating his stake has likely fallen below the disclosure threshold.

items.title: Sameera Group eyes Rs 500 crore fundraise to fuel pan-India expansion
items.summary: Sameera group is seeking Rs 500 crore for its pan-India expansion plans. The Tamil Nadu-based conglomerate recently secured substantial funding from a real estate credit platform. This capital raise aims to support their integrated township development strategy across the country. Institutional capital is increasingly supporting quality developers in the maturing real estate market. The group's diverse business interests position them for significant future growth opportunities.

items.title: Gautam Adani, family's property arm top realty wealth gains, adds Rs 38,000 crore in 2026 rankings
items.summary: Adani Properties led real estate wealth creation, adding Rs 38,000 crore to its valuation. DLF remained India's most valuable developer despite a valuation decline. Lodha Developers and Indian Hotels Company also saw valuation drops this year. The broader real estate sector experienced subdued growth, with a 2% cumulative value increase. Mumbai dominated the market, hosting fifty companies on the GROHE-Hurun India Real Estate 150 list.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
items.summary: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.

items.title: Files relating to India's largest nuclear power plant Kudankulam exposed in data breach
items.summary: Ransomware group World Leaks posted sensitive data from India's largest nuclear plant. Reliance Group confirmed a partial data breach on a third-party server. The breach could pose serious risks to the plant's safety. Indian authorities and cybersecurity agencies are investigating the incident thoroughly. This incident highlights growing cyber threats faced by Indian companies.

items.title: Skill shortage could derail India's bio-input ambitions: Agriculture Commissioner
items.summary: India's bio-input expansion faces a skilled workforce shortage. This gap requires urgent development of training programs for biological systems. The government must detail specific workforce needs and educational pathways. This initiative mirrors past AI skill development efforts. Such measures are crucial for India's natural farming ambitions.

items.title: ITC enters cola wars with B Natural Coconut Cola, bets on healthier fizz
items.summary: ITC Ltd has launched B Natural Coconut Cola, entering India's competitive beverage market. This new drink combines cola taste with coconut water for refreshment. The company targets the premium, low-sugar segment with this innovation. It contains zero caffeine and zero added sugar, offering a healthier choice. items.title: US Russia sanctions bill cuts India, China tariff threat from 500% to 100%

This revised measure aims to increase pressure on Moscow and honor Senator Lindsey Graham's legacy.

items.title: Rice, oilseed, pulses prices rise as Kharif crop sowing declines

Acreage for these crops declined significantly compared to last year. Traders are holding produce, limiting supply and pushing prices higher. Below normal rainfall further exacerbates concerns about future availability.

items.title: India bans import of goods made with forced labour

The new policy prohibits imports of goods manufactured wholly or in part through forced labour. This prohibition will take effect after a 30-day period from its official gazette publication. The government can issue notifications to ban specific goods based on evidence of forced labour.
